title: trematolobelia kaalae
intro: Trematolobelia kaalae, also known as the Ma'o hau hele, is a rare, yellow-flowering plant native to Hawaii that belongs to the Lobeliaceae family. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Trematolobelia kaalae include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and relieving respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or salve for topical application, with some herbalists recommending its use in moderation due to potential side effects.
